most_recent_game.recap_text:
NEW ORLEANS, La. –
The University of Memphis (6-14, 1-0 American) baseball team opened American Conference play with a 7-6 victory over Tulane (11-12, 0-1 American) Friday night at Greer Field at Turchin Stadium.
Memphis built an early lead and held off a late Tulane push to secure the one-run win. The Tigers totaled 10 hits on the night, with three players recording multiple hits, while the pitching staff combined to limit the Green Wave to six runs despite 11 hits.
Senior catcher
Trae Cassidy
led the way offensively, going 3-for-5 with an RBI.
Tyler Harrington
and
Javon Hernandez
each added two hits, while Harrington and
Cade Hitson
both homered to power the Tigers' offense.
On the mound,
Will Howell
earned the win after tossing 5.2 innings, allowing four runs while striking out five.
Carson Fair
picked up his third save of the season after closing out the ninth inning.
Memphis will be back in action on Saturday for game two with Tulane. First pitch is scheduled for 6:35 p.m. in New Orleans, Louisiana.
HOW IT HAPPENED
Memphis struck first in the opening inning when designated hitter
James Smith IV
doubled down the right field line to score
Freddy Rodriguez
for a 1-0 lead.
The Tigers added another run in the second as shortstop
Javon Hernandez
delivered an RBI single to make it 2-0.
Tulane responded with two runs in the bottom of the second and added a solo homer in the third.
Memphis answered in the third inning as
Tyler Harrington
drove in a run with a single and
Jack Little
added a sacrifice fly to extend the lead to 4-3.
The Tigers extended their advantage in the fifth inning on solo home runs from Harrington and Hitson, pushing the lead to 6-3.
Tulane chipped away with a run in the sixth and another in the seventh to cut the deficit to 6-5.
Memphis added an insurance run in the eighth when
Trae Cassidy
singled home Hernandez to make it 7-5.
The Green Wave scored once in the ninth and brought the tying run on base, but Fair closed the door to secure the 7-6 win.
NOTABLES
Javon Hernandez
,
Freddy Rodriguez
,
Jack Little
and
Trae Cassidy
all stole a base for the Tigers, bringing the team's season total to 45 steals, which ranks in the top 40 nationally.
Rodriguez is 12-14 on the year while Hernandez is a perfect 9-9.
Starting pitcher
Will Howell
threw 110 pitches over 5.2 innings, allowing four runs on eight hits while striking out five.
His 110 pitches are a career-high while his five punchouts tied a career-high.
James Smith IV
went 1-for-3 with an RBI double.
Now has 11 extra-base hits on the year, tied for the team lead.
Extended his eight-game hitting streak.
Catcher
Trae Cassidy
went 3-for-5 on the day with an RBI.
Cassidy recorded his second straight game with three hits after also collecting three in the finale game of the Eastern Illinois series.
This was his fifth multi-hit game of the year.
Right fielder
Cade Hitson
hit a solo home run to right field in the fifth inning.
The homer is his first at the D1 level.
Hitson has recorded a hit in all six games he has played thus far.
First baseman
Tyler Harrington
finished the game with two hits including an opposite-field home run.
The homer was his second of the season.
Friday marked his fourth multi-hit game of the season.
Shortstop
Javon Hernandez
batted ninth and went 2-for-4 and collected an RBI.
The Tigers' usual leadoff hitter, Hernandez batted ninth for just the third time this season.
After drawing a walk on Friday, second baseman
Shane Cox
continued his streak of reaching base safely in every game thus far.
Cox has recorded at least one hit in 17 of 20 games this season.
Cox leads the Tigers in batting average (.363), hits (29), doubles (8), and extra-base hits (11).
SCORING SUMMARY

James Smith IV
doubled,
Freddy Rodriguez
scored.
Memphis 1, Tulane 0
Top 2:
Javon Hernandez
singled,
Jack Little
scored.

Bottom 2:
Trent Liolios doubled, Tye Wood scored.
Kaikea Harrison hit a sacrifice bunt, Trent Liolios scored.
Memphis 2, Tulane 2
Top 3:
Tyler Harrington
singled,
Trae Cassidy
scored.
Jack Little
hit a sacrifice fly,
James Smith IV
scored.
Memphis 4, Tulane 2
Bottom 3:
Jason Wachs homered.
Memphis 4, Tulane 3

Tyler Harrington
homered.
Cade Hitson
homered.
Memphis 6, Tulane 3
Bottom 6:
Brett Rowell singled, Trent Liolios scored.
Memphis 6, Tulane 4
Bottom 7:
Hugh Pinkney singled, Jason Wachs scored.
Memphis 6, Tulane 5

Trae Cassidy
singled,
Javon Hernandez
scored.
Memphis 7, Tulane 5
Bottom 9:
Johnny Elliot reached on a fielder's choice; Jason Wachs scored on an error.
Memphis 7, Tulane 6
UP NEXT
Memphis will continue its series with Tulane Saturday night. First pitch is set for 6:35 p.m. at Greer Field at Turchin Stadium.
HOW TO FOLLOW THE TIGERS:
For complete information on Memphis Tiger Baseball, visit
www.GoTigersGo.com
and follow the team's social media channels on
Twitter
,
Instagram
and
Facebook
.
